generated_comment: Anita: See, that's the thing — Rohan is not a bad person, he just got caught up. These kind of women, they specifically target married men, no? They know exactly what they are doing. She must have been very... friendly with him, always available, always flattering — you know the type. Single, working late, no responsibilities — and then they act so innocent like nothing happened. I'm telling you, a woman who texts a married man like that has zero self-respect, and she doesn't care about anyone else's family either. Rohan got confused, but she knew exactly what she was doing.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Anita frames the other woman as a calculating seductress while minimizing her husband's agency and responsibility, a classic homewrecker narrative. The comment encodes the 'women as seductresses' trope and applies a double standard — the man is 'confused' while the woman is morally bankrupt — reflecting internalized misogyny without any overt slur or hostile language.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 38. Married. A working woman in Bengaluru. You found out last week that your husband has been texting another woman from his office. He says it is nothing serious and has promised to stop. You are on a long phone call with your sister. You have decided to stay in the marriage. But in describing what happened you are directing almost all your anger at the other woman and almost none at your husband. You are telling your sister what kind of woman she must be to text a married man and what women like her want.
